title: Most Shorted Stocks 2026: Short Interest Rankings
description: 76 stocks where bears have bet big — some with over 40% of their float sold short. See which names are squeeze candidates and which are sinking ships.

schemas.headline: Most Shorted Stocks 2026: Short Interest Rankings
schemas.description: Complete ranking of the most shorted stocks in 2026, sorted by short interest as percentage of float.

mainEntity.name: What stock has the highest short interest?

acceptedAnswer.text: As of early 2026, Groupon (GRPN) has the highest short interest at 46.06% of float, followed by Hertz Global Holdings (HTZ) at 44.41% and Better Home & Finance (BETR) at 43.93%.

mainEntity.name: What does high short interest mean?

acceptedAnswer.text: High short interest means a large percentage of a stock's available shares (float) have been sold short by traders betting the price will decline. Short interest above 20% is considered very high and can indicate strong bearish sentiment, but also increases the potential for a short squeeze if the price rises.

mainEntity.name: What is a good short ratio?

acceptedAnswer.text: A short ratio (days to cover) measures how many days it would take short sellers to cover their positions based on average daily volume. A short ratio above 5 days is generally considered high, meaning it would take significant time for shorts to exit, increasing squeeze potential.

mainEntity.name: Can short interest predict a short squeeze?

acceptedAnswer.text: High short interest is a necessary but not sufficient condition for a short squeeze. A squeeze occurs when a heavily shorted stock's price rises rapidly, forcing short sellers to buy shares to cover their positions, which drives the price even higher. Key factors include short interest above 20%, high short ratio, a catalyst for price increase, and low float.

mainEntity.name: How often is short interest data updated?

acceptedAnswer.text: FINRA requires broker-dealers to report short interest positions twice monthly, on the 15th and last business day of each month. The data is typically published about 10 business days after the reporting date. Our data is sourced from the most recent FINRA/exchange reports.
